## [00:00 - 03:04]

As Cole here, and in this video, we're going to talk about sales best practices. So tips and best practices for your sales calls. And think of it as like repriming your physiology and kind of resetting and re-centering yourself. And the reason why this is so important is that what we want to avoid is one bad call turning into two bad calls, or two bad calls, new a bad day, a call is bad day, into a bad week, bad week, into a bad month, etc. Right? We really want to limit our downside in terms of our cell slumps and make sure that each call stays in its own universe. So if we have a bad call, whether we didn't show up in the right way, or the prospect just has this really negative, nasty, like victim mindset, after every single one of those, we want to process where we can kind of take a mental shower, rinse all of that energy off, and then come into the next call as our best selves and just execute the process as good as we can. So the way we do that, and the way I like to think about it is having a routine that hits all three of these things. This is the triad from Tony Robbins Physiology, Language, and Focus. So what I've found is I want to do something that's very, very fast. I have no excuse not to do it before any call, even if I'm running a couple minutes late, I can take 60 seconds just to do this routine. So what we're going to want to do is first just take six deep breaths. So deep breaths in, hold at the top for 10 seconds, breathe out for 10 seconds, hold at the bottom for 10 seconds, and then repeat. you're getting information. It's also called the discovery. So the first principle I want you guys to know here is that the invasiveness dictates the syntax. And to an extent, I do believe that to be true, but do not coach the prospect in the discovery portion of the call. Because what happens is you're going deeper and deeper and deeper into your discovery based on asking questions about what the prospect's saying and using Socratic Dialogue like what we teach, right? Anytime you start coaching the prospect, you're pulling the conversation back up to the surface. We're going to want to ask a question that's relevant to the information that they just gave us to go deeper, that Socratic Dialogue.
